speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My desolation does begin to make

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: A better life. ’Tis paltry to be Caesar;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Not being Fortune, he’s but Fortune’s knave,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: A minister of her will. And it is great

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To do that thing that ends all other deeds,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Which shackles accidents and bolts up change,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Which sleeps and never palates more the dung,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The beggar’s nurse, and Caesar’s.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Caesar sends greeting to the Queen of Egypt,

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: And bids thee study on what fair demands

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Thou mean’st to have him grant thee.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What’s thy name?

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: My name is Proculeius.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Antony

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Did tell me of you, bade me trust you, but

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I do not greatly care to be deceived

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That have no use for trusting. If your master

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Would have a queen his beggar, you must tell him

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That majesty, to keep decorum, must

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: No less beg than a kingdom. If he please

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To give me conquered Egypt for my son,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He gives me so much of mine own as I

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Will kneel to him with thanks.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Be of good cheer.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: You’re fall’n into a princely hand; fear nothing.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Make your full reference freely to my lord,

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Who is so full of grace that it flows over

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: On all that need. Let me report to him

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Your sweet dependency, and you shall find

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: A conqueror that will pray in aid for kindness

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Where he for grace is kneeled to.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Pray you tell him

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I am his fortune’s vassal and I send him

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The greatness he has got. I hourly learn

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: A doctrine of obedience, and would gladly

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Look him i’ th’ face.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: This I’ll report, dear lady.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Have comfort, for I know your plight is pitied

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Of him that caused it.

speaker: GALLUS
text: You see how easily she may be surprised.

speaker: GALLUS
text: Guard her till Caesar come.

speaker: IRAS
text: Royal queen!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: O, Cleopatra, thou art taken, queen!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Quick, quick, good hands!

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Hold, worthy lady, hold!

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Do not yourself such wrong, who are in this

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Relieved, but not betrayed.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What, of death, too,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That rids our dogs of languish?

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Cleopatra,

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Do not abuse my master’s bounty by

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Th’ undoing of yourself. Let the world see

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: His nobleness well acted, which your death

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Will never let come forth.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Where art thou, Death?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Come hither, come! Come, come, and take a queen

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Worth many babes and beggars.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: O, temperance, lady!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Sir, I will eat no meat; I’ll not drink, sir.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If idle talk will once be necessary—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I’ll not sleep neither. This mortal house I’ll ruin,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Do Caesar what he can. Know, sir, that I

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Will not wait pinioned at your master’s court,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Nor once be chastised with the sober eye

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Of dull Octavia. Shall they hoist me up

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And show me to the shouting varletry

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Of censuring Rome? Rather a ditch in Egypt

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be gentle grave unto me; rather on Nilus’ mud

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Lay me stark naked, and let the waterflies

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Blow me into abhorring; rather make

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My country’s high pyramides my gibbet

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And hang me up in chains!

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: You do extend

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: These thoughts of horror further than you shall

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Find cause in Caesar.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Proculeius,

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: What thou hast done thy master Caesar knows,

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: And he hath sent for thee. For the Queen,

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: I’ll take her to my guard.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: So, Dolabella,

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: It shall content me best. Be gentle to her.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text:
To
                 
                Cleopatra
                .
                 
              To Caesar I will speak what you shall please,

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: If you’ll employ me to him.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Say I would die.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Most noble empress, you have heard of me.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I cannot tell.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Assuredly you know me.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: No matter, sir, what I have heard or known.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: You laugh when boys or women tell their dreams;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is ’t not your trick?

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: I understand not, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I dreamt there was an emperor Antony.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, such another sleep, that I might see

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But such another man.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: If it might please you—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: His face was as the heavens, and therein stuck

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: A sun and moon, which kept their course and lighted

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The little O, the Earth.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Most sovereign creature—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: His legs bestrid the ocean, his reared arm

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Crested the world. His voice was propertied

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As all the tunèd spheres, and that to friends;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But when he meant to quail and shake the orb,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He was as rattling thunder. For his bounty,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: There was no winter in ’t; an autumn ’twas

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That grew the more by reaping. His delights

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Were dolphin-like; they showed his back above

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The element they lived in. In his livery

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Walked crowns and crownets; realms and islands were

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As plates dropped from his pocket.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Cleopatra—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Think you there was, or might be, such a man

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As this I dreamt of?

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Gentle madam, no.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: You lie up to the hearing of the gods!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But if there be nor ever were one such,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: It’s past the size of dreaming. Nature wants stuff

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To vie strange forms with fancy, yet t’ imagine

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: An Antony were nature’s piece ’gainst fancy,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Condemning shadows quite.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Hear me, good madam.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Your loss is as yourself, great; and you bear it

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: As answering to the weight. Would I might never

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: O’ertake pursued success but I do feel,

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: By the rebound of yours, a grief that smites

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: My very heart at root.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I thank you, sir.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Know you what Caesar means to do with me?

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: I am loath to tell you what I would you knew.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Nay, pray you, sir.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Though he be honorable—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He’ll lead me, then, in triumph.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Madam, he will. I know ’t.

speaker: ALL
text: Make way there! Caesar!

speaker: CAESAR
text: Which is the Queen of Egypt?

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: It is the Emperor, madam.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Arise. You shall not kneel.

speaker: CAESAR
text: I pray you, rise. Rise, Egypt.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Sir, the gods

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Will have it thus. My master and my lord

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I must obey.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Take to you no hard thoughts.

speaker: CAESAR
text: The record of what injuries you did us,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Though written in our flesh, we shall remember

speaker: CAESAR
text: As things but done by chance.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Sole sir o’ th’ world,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I cannot project mine own cause so well

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To make it clear, but do confess I have

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Been laden with like frailties which before

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Have often shamed our sex.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Cleopatra, know

speaker: CAESAR
text: We will extenuate rather than enforce.

speaker: CAESAR
text: If you apply yourself to our intents,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Which towards you are most gentle, you shall find

speaker: CAESAR
text: A benefit in this change; but if you seek

speaker: CAESAR
text: To lay on me a cruelty by taking

speaker: CAESAR
text: Antony’s course, you shall bereave yourself

speaker: CAESAR
text: Of my good purposes, and put your children

speaker: CAESAR
text: To that destruction which I’ll guard them from

speaker: CAESAR
text: If thereon you rely. I’ll take my leave.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And may through all the world. ’Tis yours, and we,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Your scutcheons and your signs of conquest, shall

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Hang in what place you please. Here, my good lord.

speaker: CAESAR
text: You shall advise me in all for Cleopatra.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: This is the brief of money, plate, and jewels

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I am possessed of. ’Tis exactly valued,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Not petty things admitted.—Where’s Seleucus?

speaker: SELEUCUS
text: Here, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: This is my treasurer. Let him speak, my lord,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Upon his peril, that I have reserved

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To myself nothing.—Speak the truth, Seleucus.

speaker: SELEUCUS
text: Madam, I had rather seel my lips

speaker: SELEUCUS
text: Than to my peril speak that which is not.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What have I kept back?

speaker: SELEUCUS
text: Enough to purchase what you have made known.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Nay, blush not, Cleopatra. I approve

speaker: CAESAR
text: Your wisdom in the deed.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: See, Caesar, O, behold

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: How pomp is followed! Mine will now be yours,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And should we shift estates, yours would be mine.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The ingratitude of this Seleucus does

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Even make me wild.—O slave, of no more trust

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Than love that’s hired! What, goest thou back? Thou shalt

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Go back, I warrant thee! But I’ll catch thine eyes

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Though they had wings. Slave, soulless villain, dog!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O rarely base!

speaker: CAESAR
text: Good queen, let us entreat you—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O Caesar, what a wounding shame is this,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That thou vouchsafing here to visit me,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Doing the honor of thy lordliness

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To one so meek, that mine own servant should

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Parcel the sum of my disgraces by

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Addition of his envy! Say, good Caesar,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That I some lady trifles have reserved,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Immoment toys, things of such dignity

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As we greet modern friends withal, and say

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Some nobler token I have kept apart

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: For Livia and Octavia, to induce

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Their mediation, must I be unfolded

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: With one that I have bred? The gods! It smites me

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text:
Beneath the fall I have.
                 
                To
                 
                Seleucus
                .
               Prithee, go hence,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Or I shall show the cinders of my spirits

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Through th’ ashes of my chance. Wert thou a man,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou wouldst have mercy on me.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Forbear, Seleucus.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be it known that we, the greatest, are misthought

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: For things that others do; and when we fall,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: We answer others’ merits in our name—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Are therefore to be pitied.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Cleopatra,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Not what you have reserved nor what acknowledged

speaker: CAESAR
text: Put we i’ th’ roll of conquest. Still be ’t yours!

speaker: CAESAR
text: Bestow it at your pleasure, and believe

speaker: CAESAR
text: Caesar’s no merchant to make prize with you

speaker: CAESAR
text: Of things that merchants sold. Therefore be cheered.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Make not your thoughts your prisons. No, dear queen,

speaker: CAESAR
text: For we intend so to dispose you as

speaker: CAESAR
text: Yourself shall give us counsel. Feed and sleep.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Our care and pity is so much upon you

speaker: CAESAR
text: That we remain your friend. And so adieu.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My master and my lord!

speaker: CAESAR
text: Not so. Adieu.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He words me, girls, he words me, that I should not

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be noble to myself. But hark thee, Charmian.

speaker: IRAS
text: Finish, good lady. The bright day is done,

speaker: IRAS
text: And we are for the dark.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Hie thee again.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I have spoke already, and it is provided.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Go put it to the haste.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Madam, I will.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Where’s the Queen?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Behold, sir.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Dolabella.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Madam, as thereto sworn by your command,

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Which my love makes religion to obey,

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: I tell you this: Caesar through Syria

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Intends his journey, and within three days

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: You with your children will he send before.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Make your best use of this. I have performed

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Your pleasure and my promise.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Dolabella,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I shall remain your debtor.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: I your servant.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Adieu, good queen. I must attend on Caesar.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Farewell, and thanks.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Now, Iras, what think’st thou?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou an Egyptian puppet shall be shown

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: In Rome as well as I. Mechanic slaves

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: With greasy aprons, rules, and hammers shall

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Uplift us to the view. In their thick breaths,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Rank of gross diet, shall we be enclouded

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And forced to drink their vapor.

speaker: IRAS
text: The gods forbid!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Nay, ’tis most certain, Iras. Saucy lictors

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Will catch at us like strumpets, and scald rhymers

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ballad us out o’ tune. The quick comedians

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Extemporally will stage us and present

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Our Alexandrian revels. Antony

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Shall be brought drunken forth, and I shall see

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Some squeaking Cleopatra boy my greatness

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I’ th’ posture of a whore.

speaker: IRAS
text: O the good gods!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Nay, that’s certain.

speaker: IRAS
text: I’ll never see ’t! For I am sure mine nails

speaker: IRAS
text: Are stronger than mine eyes.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Why, that’s the way

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To fool their preparation and to conquer

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Their most absurd intents.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Now, Charmian!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Show me, my women, like a queen. Go fetch

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My best attires. I am again for Cydnus

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To meet Mark Antony. Sirrah Iras, go.—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Now, noble Charmian, we’ll dispatch indeed,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And when thou hast done this chare, I’ll give thee leave

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To play till Doomsday.—Bring our crown and all.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Wherefore’s this noise?

speaker: GUARDSMAN
text: Here is a rural fellow

speaker: GUARDSMAN
text: That will not be denied your Highness’ presence.

speaker: GUARDSMAN
text: He brings you figs.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Let him come in.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What poor an instrument

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: May do a noble deed! He brings me liberty.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My resolution’s placed, and I have nothing

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Of woman in me. Now from head to foot

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I am marble-constant. Now the fleeting moon

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: No planet is of mine.

speaker: GUARDSMAN
text: This is the man.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Avoid, and leave him.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Hast thou the pretty worm of Nilus there

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That kills and pains not?

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: Truly I have him, but I would not be

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: the party that should desire you to touch him, for

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: his biting is immortal. Those that do die of it do

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: seldom or never recover.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Remember’st thou any that have died on ’t?

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: Very many, men and women too. I

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: heard of one of them no longer than yesterday—a

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: very honest woman, but something given to lie, as a

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: woman should not do but in the way of honesty—

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: how she died of the biting of it, what pain she felt.

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: Truly, she makes a very good report o’ th’ worm.

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: But he that will believe all that they say shall never

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: be saved by half that they do. But this is most

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: falliable, the worm’s an odd worm.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Get thee hence. Farewell.

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: I wish you all joy of the worm.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Farewell.

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: You must think this, look you, that the

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: worm will do his kind.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ay, ay, farewell.

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: Look you, the worm is not to be trusted

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: but in the keeping of wise people, for indeed there

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: is no goodness in the worm.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Take thou no care; it shall be heeded.

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: Very good. Give it nothing, I pray you,

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: for it is not worth the feeding.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Will it eat me?

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: You must not think I am so simple but

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: I know the devil himself will not eat a woman. I

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: know that a woman is a dish for the gods if the devil

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: dress her not. But truly these same whoreson devils

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: do the gods great harm in their women, for in every

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: ten that they make, the devils mar five.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Well, get thee gone. Farewell.

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: Yes, forsooth. I wish you joy o’ th’

speaker: COUNTRYMAN
text: worm.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Give me my robe. Put on my crown. I have

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Immortal longings in me. Now no more

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The juice of Egypt’s grape shall moist this lip.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Yare, yare, good Iras, quick. Methinks I hear

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Antony call. I see him rouse himself

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To praise my noble act. I hear him mock

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The luck of Caesar, which the gods give men

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To excuse their after wrath.—Husband, I come!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Now to that name my courage prove my title.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I am fire and air; my other elements

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I give to baser life.—So, have you done?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Come then, and take the last warmth of my lips.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Farewell, kind Charmian.—Iras, long farewell.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Have I the aspic in my lips? Dost fall?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If thou and nature can so gently part,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The stroke of death is as a lover’s pinch,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Which hurts and is desired. Dost thou lie still?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If thus thou vanishest, thou tell’st the world

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: It is not worth leave-taking.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Dissolve, thick cloud, and rain, that I may say

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: The gods themselves do weep!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: This proves me base.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If she first meet the curlèd Antony,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He’ll make demand of her, and spend that kiss

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Which is my heaven to have.—Come, thou mortal wretch,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: With thy sharp teeth this knot intrinsicate

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Of life at once untie. Poor venomous fool,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be angry and dispatch. O, couldst thou speak,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That I might hear thee call great Caesar ass

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Unpolicied!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: O eastern star!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Peace, peace!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Dost thou not see my baby at my breast,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That sucks the nurse asleep?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: O, break! O, break!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As sweet as balm, as soft as air, as gentle—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O Antony!—Nay, I will take thee too.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What should I stay—

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: In this wild world? So, fare thee well.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Now boast thee, Death, in thy possession lies

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: A lass unparalleled. Downy windows, close,

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: And golden Phoebus, never be beheld

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Of eyes again so royal. Your crown’s awry.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: I’ll mend it, and then play—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: Where’s the Queen?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Speak softly. Wake her not.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: Caesar hath sent—

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Too slow a messenger.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: O, come apace, dispatch! I partly feel thee.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: Approach, ho! All’s not well. Caesar’s beguiled.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GUARD
text: There’s Dolabella sent from Caesar. Call him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: What work is here, Charmian? Is this well done?

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: It is well done, and fitting for a princess

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Descended of so many royal kings.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Ah, soldier!

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: How goes it here?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GUARD
text: All dead.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Caesar, thy thoughts

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Touch their effects in this. Thyself art coming

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: To see performed the dreaded act which thou

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: So sought’st to hinder.

speaker: ALL
text: A way there, a way for Caesar!

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: O sir, you are too sure an augurer:

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: That you did fear is done.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Bravest at the last,

speaker: CAESAR
text: She leveled at our purposes and, being royal,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Took her own way. The manner of their deaths?

speaker: CAESAR
text: I do not see them bleed.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Who was last with them?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: A simple countryman that brought her figs.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: This was his basket.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Poisoned, then.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: O Caesar,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: This Charmian lived but now; she stood and spake.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: I found her trimming up the diadem

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: On her dead mistress; tremblingly she stood,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: And on the sudden dropped.

speaker: CAESAR
text: O, noble weakness!

speaker: CAESAR
text: If they had swallowed poison, ’twould appear

speaker: CAESAR
text: By external swelling; but she looks like sleep,

speaker: CAESAR
text: As she would catch another Antony

speaker: CAESAR
text: In her strong toil of grace.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Here on her breast

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: There is a vent of blood, and something blown.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: The like is on her arm.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: This is an aspic’s trail, and these fig leaves

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: Have slime upon them, such as th’ aspic leaves

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GUARD
text: Upon the caves of Nile.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Most probable

speaker: CAESAR
text: That so she died, for her physician tells me

speaker: CAESAR
text: She hath pursued conclusions infinite

speaker: CAESAR
text: Of easy ways to die. Take up her bed,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And bear her women from the monument.

speaker: CAESAR
text: She shall be buried by her Antony.

speaker: CAESAR
text: No grave upon the earth shall clip in it

speaker: CAESAR
text: A pair so famous. High events as these

speaker: CAESAR
text: Strike those that make them; and their story is

speaker: CAESAR
text: No less in pity than his glory which

speaker: CAESAR
text: Brought them to be lamented. Our army shall

speaker: CAESAR
text: In solemn show attend this funeral,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And then to Rome. Come, Dolabella, see

speaker: CAESAR
text: High order in this great solemnity.
